title: sophora toromiro
intro: Sophora toromiro, also known as the Easter Island tree, is a plant species native to Easter Island, now extinct in the wild. Traditionally, the plant's roots, stems, and leaves were used by the indigenous Rapa Nui people for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Sophora toromiro are said to include treating respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. Historically, the plant was used in herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, as well as being used to make a medicinal paste, but due to its extinction, these uses are now largely theoretical.
